text: Good morning. My morning routine with my seven month old begins when he wakes up. I get
to hear Weston on the baby monitor and then I go check him. As you can hear, he's awake.
Good morning, healthy sleep. Did you sleep good? Let's get you changed. Now it's the first
change of the day because who wants a dirty diaper? We make sure that he is safely on his changing
table and we have all of our supplies. It's great in our drawer here. We have our wipes and our
diapers. So let's get him changed. We are all changed and now we're going to make breakfast. We
like to make some pancakes so he can get some practice. Weston goes into his
high chair so we're going to put him down and buckle him in. After breakfast, we like to go
for a morning walk to get some sun. We have our stroller, my coffee and of course Weston and our
dog. We're off for a nice stroll around the neighborhood. And that is my morning routine with a
seven month old. But because he's so young, it changes every day so we just roll with it.
A nighttime routine with a seven month old can vary sometimes. But for tonight, our nighttime routine
is starting with some dinner. We are practicing our salad foods. I am using an avocado
and doing little slices for him so he can practice eating. We just give it to him so it's kind of
like baby wide wing. Grab it. Yay. Good bite. After dinner, we like to take our bath because he gets
extra messy to clean with his food. We have his towel and his washcloth ready to go. We're going to
go get his st. tug and get him in the bath. Start for start. Which one? Oh the star, that's a good one.
That's amazing. Now that bath time is all done, we are going to dry him off. Get a onesie on him
and then come make a bottle for the night. We are all dressed for bed so now I'm going to make his
bottle. We'll dab rocks with him for a little bit. We use similac and a doctor runs glass bottle.
We give him seven and a half ounces in here. We'll get a nice hot cup of water. The cup of water
is to warm it up so it's not too hot but just warm. Let it sit for a bit while we go and finish
playing with him. Once it's warm, then we'll feed, breastfeed and light out for bedtime.
We are all ready for bedtime in our nighttime bottle. First, we test it to make sure it's not too hot.
I just dump a little on my wrist and if it feels fine to me, not too cold and not too hot,
we are good to go and we're ready. Good night everybody.
